title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “If everything is permitted, then everything is hateful.”
action: Establish core values to prevent systemic collapse into nihilism.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
content: “Of all the creatures that breathe and move upon the earth, nothing is bred that is weaker than man.”
action: Acknowledge your fragility relative to large-scale environmental forces.

title: Waiting for Godot
author: Samuel Beckett
content: “They give birth astride of a grave, the light gleams an instant, then it's night once more.”
action: Operate with an acute awareness of your temporal limitations.

title: Inferno
author: Dante Alighieri
content: “The hottest places in hell are reserved for those who in time of moral crisis preserve their neutrality.”
action: Recognize that maintaining neutrality is a definitive and consequential act.

title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
content: “I opened myself to the gentle indifference of the world.”
action: Align your internal state with the objective neutrality of your environment.
